content.thinking:
Now I understand the situation. From the precondition log, I can see:
1. A 3-node Elasticsearch cluster (`es-cluster-0`, `es-cluster-1`, `es-cluster-2`) was deployed in the `elasticsearch` namespace
2. The ConfigMap `es-config` was modified with wrong `discovery.seed_hosts` - they point to `default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`
3. Then `es-cluster-2` was deleted (scale to 2), its PVC was deleted, and then scaled back to 3
4. After waiting for 2 nodes to stabilize, `es-cluster-2` is the one that can't rejoin because the seed hosts configuration is pointing to the wrong namespace domain names. The ConfigMap specifies hosts in `default.svc.cluster.local` when they should be in `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`, so the new pod can't discover the existing cluster members.

My approach is to update the ConfigMap with the correct seed host addresses, then restart the problematic pod so it picks up the fixed configuration and rejoins the cluster successfully. Let me start by checking the current cluster state and kubeconfig.
Now I understand the full setup. Let me check the current state of the cluster. First, let me set up KUBECONFIG to use the bundle kubeconfig.

content.text: Now I understand the fault: the ConfigMap was corrupted with wrong namespace in seed_hosts (`default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`), then `es-cluster-2` was restarted with its PVC deleted, so it can't discover the cluster. Let me verify and fix it.

content.thinking:
I can see the issue clearly:
1. The ConfigMap has wrong seed hosts: `es-cluster-0.es-cluster.default.svc.cluster.local` but they should be `es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`
2. Only 2 nodes (es-cluster-0, es-cluster-1) are in the cluster; es-cluster-2 can't join

The fix:
1. Update the ConfigMap with the correct namespace in discovery.seed_hosts
2. Restart es-cluster-2 so it picks up the corrected config
3. The first two nodes started before the ConfigMap was corrupted, so they have the correct cluster state already established. When I fix the ConfigMap and restart es-cluster-2, it should be able to discover and join the existing cluster using the corrected seed hosts.

Let me look at the original resource files to verify what the correct elasticsearch.yml configuration should be.

content.text: The ConfigMap has wrong seed hosts (`default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`). `es-cluster-2` restarted with a corrupted config and can't discover the cluster. I need to also check the original resource to get the correct config.

content.thinking:
The correct seed_hosts format is just `es-cluster-0.es-cluster` (short form, using Kubernetes DNS with the headless service). The corrupted version uses the full FQDN but with the wrong namespace (`default` instead of `elasticsearch`).

The correct fix is to restore the ConfigMap to use the short-form seed hosts: `es-cluster-0.es-cluster`, `es-cluster-1.es-cluster`, `es-cluster-2.es-cluster`.

Now let me fix the ConfigMap and then delete/restart es-cluster-2 pod so it picks up the new config.
Since es-cluster-2 is already in a restart loop, it'll pick up the updated ConfigMap when it restarts next—the volume will be refreshed as part of the pod initialization.

Now I need to update the ConfigMap with the correct seed_hosts configuration.

content.text: The correct seed_hosts use the short headless-service form (`es-cluster-0.es-cluster`). Now I'll fix the ConfigMap and force `es-cluster-2` to restart with the correct config.

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rererererun/elasticsearch-incident-response-chain-20260625_194301/bundle/kubeconfig

echo "---"
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s "http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/nodes?h=name,ip,node.role,master"
echo "---"
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s "http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/health?pretty"
